entry.title: Introducing Scale: SurrealDB Cloud, built for high availability and scale
entry.summary: SurrealDB Cloud now has a production-grade tier: Scale. Built for workloads that can't go down, Scale delivers highly-available, fault-tolerant clusters across multiple availability zones with horizontal scaling - powered by SurrealDS, SurrealDB's Gen 3 distributed storage engine with compute–storage separation and leaderless quorum consensus. On the roadmap: object-storage backing, cross-region replication, instant recovery, and instant branching.

entry.title: Bring SurrealDB to your Replit Agent
entry.summary: SurrealDB's MCP server lets Replit Agent build apps directly on a database you own, reading your schema and querying live data.

entry.title: Social graphs for activity-tracking apps
entry.summary: Build social graphs in SurrealDB: model followers, kudos, segments, and clubs as one graph, with friend-of-friend suggestions and leaderboards in a single query.

entry.title: Build apps on your data with SurrealDB and Lovable
entry.summary: Connect SurrealDB to Lovable using the Model Context Protocol (MCP), so the Lovable Agent can read your schema, query your live data, and build a working application on top of it without you writing any glue code.

entry.title: A living knowledge layer for your agents: SurrealDB + CocoIndex
entry.summary: Announcing the native SurrealDB connector for CocoIndex — build a continuously-fresh document store, knowledge graph, and vector index in a single database, from one declarative pipeline.

entry.title: Deterministic simulation testing and its use at SurrealDB
entry.summary: How deterministic simulation works from easy to complex scenarios, and its ussage at SurrealDB's transactional key-value store and embedded key-value engine.

entry.title: What's new in Surrealist 3.9
entry.summary: Surrealist 3.9 introduces a complete design overhaul, a new datasets browser and data manager, and loads of other enhancements.

entry.title: Organizations and Teams for your SurrealDB App
entry.summary: Bootstrapping multi-tenant orgs, roles, permissions, and the functions that enforce them - straight from SurrealKit templates.

entry.title: Fuzz testing for SurrealDB: using randomised input to find bugs before users do
entry.summary: A good choice to complement unit and integration tests, fuzz testing lets you use semi-random data to discover obscure bugs before users do.

entry.title: SurrealDB is now available on the Nebius AI Cloud Marketplace
entry.summary: SurrealDB is now available on the Nebius AI Cloud Marketplace.

entry.title: Context layers, semantic layers, and knowledge graphs: the modern data architecture for AI
entry.summary: Three concepts AI teams constantly confuse: a context layer assembles the right data into an LLM prompt at query time, a semantic layer maps raw data to business meaning, and a knowledge graph stores entities and their relationships. Most teams build all three by stitching together separate graph, vector, and relational systems - creating sync problems, higher latency, and rising costs. SurrealDB's multi-model architecture handles all three natively, letting a single SurrealQL query run vector search, graph traversal, and document retrieval in one round trip

entry.title: SurrealDB 3.x by the numbers
entry.summary: Fresh SurrealDB 3.x benchmarks: 141k ops\/s CRUD, scans 164× faster than 2.x, and head-to-head numbers vs. Postgres, Mongo, Neo4j, and Redis.

entry.title: SurrealDB 3.1: stability, DiskANN, and a new release process
entry.summary: SurrealDB 3.1 is here. The first minor release in the 3.x series brings DiskANN, a comprehensive stability and security pass, and a refreshed release process.

entry.title: Using SurrealDB to understand how Buffalo buffalo buffalo Buffalo buffalo
entry.summary: Using SurrealDB and Surrealist's graph visualisation to not just understand but also see how Buffalo buffalo Buffalo buffalo buffalo buffalo Buffalo buffalo works.

entry.title: Agentic retrieval for structured data with text-to-surql
entry.summary: RAG pipelines usually focus on unstructured content (chunks + vectors\/BM25), but structured data introduces a different retrieval problem: you need to pull exact rows and aggregates from tables, not just “similar passages.” This article explains an agentic pattern for structured retrieval using text-to-SurrealQL: give an LLM agent a single database-aware tool that converts natural-language questions into valid SurrealQL, executes the query in SurrealDB, and returns deterministic, auditable results.

entry.title: What chunking strategies exist and how to choose one?
entry.summary: If you've decided to chunk your documents for a RAG pipeline or semantic search system, the next question hits almost immediately: which chunking strategy should I use?

entry.title: What is the recommended chunk size?
entry.summary: If you're building a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline, a semantic search system, or any AI application that reads from a vector store, one question comes up almost immediately: what chunk size should I use?

entry.title: Schema migrations in SurrealDB: A local dev workflow
entry.summary: Managing schema changes in SurrealDB shouldn't slow down your local development. This post walks through a practical migration workflow that keeps your database in sync as your project evolves.

entry.title: Kreuzberg & SurrealDB: from unstructured documents to hybrid retrieval
entry.summary: SurrealDB now integrates with Kreuzberg to ingest, chunk, and search unstructured documents across 88+ formats - with built-in keyword, semantic, and hybrid search with reranking.

entry.title: Hybrid search inside SurrealDB
entry.summary: How I fused vector and keyword retrieval in a single query

entry.title: Building compounding memory with knowledge graphs and agentic RAG
entry.summary: Synapse, a memory-first reflection agent, helps users track therapy journal patterns. It structures insights into a knowledge graph, revealing patterns and answering questions based on context.

entry.title: New SurrealDB docs search using hybrid search and HNSW\/BM25 reranking
entry.summary: How SurrealDB has implemented documentation search using hybrid search (full-text and vector hybrid search and reranking with HNSW and BM25 indexes), plus a simplified example to show the same pattern in just a few queries.

entry.title: What's new in Surrealist 3.8
entry.summary: Surrealist 3.8 introduces a fully redesigned organisation view, improved query formatting, and loads of other enhancements.

entry.title: Graph RAG does not need a graph database. It needs a database that does everything.
entry.summary: Graph RAG is the right idea. Using relationships between entities to scope and improve retrieval produces better results than vector similarity alone. What matters in production is whether graph traversal, vector search, full-text search, and structured filters compose in a single atomic statement.

entry.title: How to get near-perfect, deterministic accuracy from your AI agents
entry.summary: Agent accuracy problems are almost always retrieval problems, not model problems. Scope-first retrieval and reasoning and retrieval graph feedback loops close the gap to 99%+.

entry.title: SurrealMX: In-memory storage with time travel and persistent storage
entry.summary: SurrealMX lets you use in-memory storage along with optional time travel versioned queries and two types of persistent storage with a large number of configurations to balance performance with durability.

entry.title: Exponential cost traps in database architectures: how SurrealDB breaks the cycle
entry.summary: SurrealDB offers a consolidated solution to store data once and querying it across models to reduce overall costs, particularly in storage-heavy environments where cloud block storage is in use.

entry.title: Where SurrealDB fits in your stack
entry.summary: By consolidating databases into one platform, SurrealDB enables applications with multi-model capabilities, blending relational queries, graph traversals, vector search, time-series and geospatial analysis without the overhead of disparate tools.

entry.title: SurrealDB is now available on AWS Marketplace
entry.summary: You can now deploy SurrealDB directly into your AWS environment with streamlined procurement, simplified billing, and faster time to production - all through your existing AWS environment.

entry.title: OpenAI’s Postgres Architecture: A Brilliant Fix for a Billion-Dollar Mistake
entry.summary: OpenAI engineered an impressive PostgreSQL scaling strategy, but this article argues it is a cautionary tale and explains why distributed-first architectures can avoid many of the same pitfalls.

entry.title: How to use Surrealism to build your own custom SurrealDB extensions
entry.summary: Surrealism lets you build your own custom plugins, WASM functions that you can build and compile to their own binaries that can be called directly from the database

entry.title: File support in SurrealDB 3.0
entry.summary: SurrealDB 3.0 includes experimental file support that allows you to create, update, and delete files regardless of whether the database itself saves its data persistently or not.

entry.title: Introducing JavaScript SDK 2.0
entry.summary: The most significant update to the JavaScript SDK to date: SurrealDB 3.0 support, multi-session support, automatic token refreshing, a redesigned live query API, and a new query builder pattern.

entry.title: Custom API Endpoints: streamlining your architecture
entry.summary: Custom API endpoints allow developers to define database behaviours, set up middleware, and create custom API endpoints directly within the familiar SurrealQL query language.

entry.title: SurrealDB Raises $23M Series A extension to power the AI-native database era
entry.summary: SurrealDB Raises $23M Series A extension to power the AI-native database era

entry.title: Introducing SurrealDB 3.0 - the future of AI agent memory
entry.summary: Introducing SurrealDB 3.0 - the future of AI agent memory

entry.title: SurrealDB 3.0 benchmarks: a new foundation for performance
entry.summary: SurrealDB 3.0 introduces a new benchmarking foundation that showcases its improved, production-ready performance and the architectural advancements driving it.

entry.title: Introducing Surrealism
entry.summary: Introducing Surrealism

entry.title: SurrealDB vs. Neo4j
entry.summary: SurrealDB is built for live, large-scale application data. Neo4j is built for traversal-heavy graph workloads at modest scale.

entry.title: SurrealDB vs. Postgres
entry.summary: SurrealDB natively unifies relational, graph, vector, document, and temporal data in a single engine, no extensions or workarounds.

entry.title: SurrealDB vs. MongoDB
entry.summary: SurrealDB is a unified, transactional, multi-model database. MongoDB is a document-oriented database with multiple specialised subsystems.

entry.title: How to build a knowledge graph for AI
entry.summary: What is a knowledge graph and how can it be used to enhance AI agents?

entry.title: Knowledge Graph RAG: two query patterns for smarter AI agents
entry.summary: A post that walks through two powerful SurrealQL query patterns that demonstrate how to retrieve context from a knowledge graph to feed AI agents.

entry.title: A look at SurrealQL and how it differs from PostgreSQL
entry.summary: In this article, we will explore the similarities and differences between PostgreSQL and SurrealQL. We will also see how SurrealQL can overcome some of the limitations that relational databases have.

entry.title: Learning through building: SurrealDB University's newest tutorial
entry.summary: Now you can learn SurrealDB by building your own database that takes advantage of its multi-model capabilities to store and query over 100 of the world's most popular movies.

entry.title: Agents with memory: how Agno and SurrealDB enable reliable AI systems
entry.summary: Highlights from our Agno x SurrealDB livestream on building reliable, context-rich agents with a strong memory layer.

entry.title: PolyAI on building context-aware voice agents: latency, knowledge bases, and what actually ships
entry.summary: Key takeaways from SurrealDB Stream #34 with PolyAI CTO & co-founder Shawn Wen on the hard parts of shipping context-aware voice agents: latency budgets, knowledge governance, and operational trust.

entry.title: SurrealDB joins the NVIDIA Inception Program
entry.summary: SurrealDB joins the NVIDIA Inception Program, gaining access to GPU acceleration, CUDA integrations, and advanced vector search capabilities for AI-driven applications.

entry.title: Why companies are adopting SurrealDB
entry.summary: SurrealDB unifies documents, graphs, vectors, and real-time logic in one engine, cutting infrastructure complexity and empowering faster, more flexible development for modern AI-driven applications.

entry.title: Exploring the new SurrealDB integration with Agno
entry.summary: SurrealDB's built-in capabilities comprising graph queries, geolocational data and recursive queries make it the perfect fit to identify malevolent entities involved in organised influence campaigns.
